title: ChamferFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing chamfer features in a component and supports the ability to create new chamfer features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: **RETIRED** Creates a ChamferFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the chamfer you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ChamferFeatureInput object.

methods.name: createInput2
methods.description: Creates a ChamferFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the chamfer you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ChamferFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified chamfer feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified chamfer feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of chamfer features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Equal Distance Chamfer Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates an equal distance chamfer on the selected edge. If there are tangent contiguous edges that will also be included in the chamfer.

add.title: ChamferFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created ChamferFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A ChamferFeatureInput object that defines the desired chamfer. Use the createInput method to create a new ChamferFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the ChamferFeatureInput object) to define the chamfer.

related_samples.name: chamferFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the chamferFeatures.add method. To use this sample have a part open that contains a body. When you run the script, you will be prompted to select an edge to chamfer.

related_samples.name: Equal Distance Chamfer Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates an equal distance chamfer on the selected edge. If there are tangent contiguous edges that will also be included in the chamfer.

classType.title: ChamferFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: ChamferFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Creates a ChamferFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the chamfer you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ChamferFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created ChamferFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: edges
parameters.description: The collection of edges that will be chamfered.

parameters.name: isTangentChain
parameters.description: Boolean indicating if all edges that are tangentially connected to any of the input edges should be included in the chamfer or not.

createInput2.title: ChamferFeatures.createInput2 Method
createInput2.description: Creates a ChamferFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the chamfer you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ChamferFeatureInput object.
createInput2.return_description: Returns the newly created ChamferFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

related_samples.name: chamferFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the chamferFeatures.add method. To use this sample have a part open that contains a body. When you run the script, you will be prompted to select an edge to chamfer.

related_samples.name: Equal Distance Chamfer Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates an equal distance chamfer on the selected edge. If there are tangent contiguous edges that will also be included in the chamfer.

item.title: ChamferFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified chamfer feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: ChamferFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified chamfer feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: ChamferFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of chamfer features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = chamferFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: ChamferFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = chamferFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: ChamferFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
